element_introduction_order.element: Policy sliders (7 areas: tax, economy, welfare, foreign policy, transport, law and order, public services)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn to adjust sliders while balancing voter faction satisfaction

element_introduction_order.element: Voter factions (religious, patriotic, parents, capitalists, socialists, liberals, conservatives, etc.)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Understanding multi-faction overlaps (e.g., Poor Conservative Smoker Patriot) drives strategic decision-making
Players must deliver on two promises per election cycle or face cynicism increases

element_introduction_order.element: Political capital system (earned by loyal ministers)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Resource constraint for implementing policy changes
Tax levels must avoid triggering tax evasion while funding services
Players experience passive gains/losses without direct control
Mandatory choice-based decisions that must resolve each turn

element_introduction_order.element: Situations (ongoing conditions like pollution or homelessness)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players must actively manage persistent problems or suffer voter dissatisfaction

typical_relief_triggers: resolving a situation or dilemma favorably
typical_tension_triggers: budget imbalance forces difficult policy trade-offs
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based with periodic election checkpoints; tension builds across election cycles as unmet promises accumulate cynicism

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like period of nation selection and initial policy introduction; learning voter faction overlaps and basic resource constraints
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Managing first election cycles while balancing multiple competing faction demands; cynicism accumulation becomes strategic concern
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex multi-turn planning to fulfill promises, manage unforeseen situations, and maintain voter coalition stability
notable_spikes: first election cycle (players must deliver on promises or face cynicism penalty)
late-game cascading crises (multiple situations and dilemmas coinciding with approaching election)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each voter belongs to multiple overlapping factions. Pleasing one faction often antagonizes another. Policy decisions must navigate impossible contradictions (e.g., satisfying both capitalists and socialists)
Turn sequence culminates in election with two player-made promises. Non-fulfillment triggers cynicism increase. Creates recurring tension-resolution loop
Ongoing problems (petrol protests, homelessness, pollution) persist and worsen unless addressed via policy, creating sustained pressure until resolved or mitigated
Limited political capital from ministers forces prioritization of policy changes; budget constraints prevent funding all services at desired levels
